text:
Once upon a quiet town lived a girl named Lily. Every evening, she would sit on her porch, rocking gently in her old wooden chair, listening to the wind flutter through the leaves. Her favorite song was a distant lullaby her mother sang when she was a little girl.

One night, while Lost Woods lingered in her memory, Lily heard a tiny, playful voice singing. She looked around but saw nothing. Then, a flutter beside her. It was a hummingbird. They chatted for hours. The bird taught her bird language, and they spent an enchanted evening. Lily felt something she never had before—a connection to the wild.

The next morning, Lily set out early to visit the hummingbird. She found it flitting about a fragrant bush. After a few interactions, she learned its secret song. She hummed back earnestly and received its trust. The bird led her to a secret glade, where trees swayed gently to its tune.
